description:
You are working in the project directory
    /home/user/projects/clean-sensor-data.

    1. A plain-text file named VERSION currently contains a single line with the
       string “0.2.1”.
    2. A Markdown file named CHANGELOG.md already exists and starts with the
       line “# Changelog”, followed by historical entries for versions
       0.2.0 and 0.2.1.
    3. A bug fix has just been merged that solves issue #23 (“Handle missing
       ‘temperature’ column”).  
       We now need to publish a new patch release 0.2.2 and record it
       properly.

    Goal
    ----
    Bump the semantic version from 0.2.1 to 0.2.2 and document the change.

    Required end-state of the system
    --------------------------------
    After you finish, the filesystem must look like this:

    • /home/user/projects/clean-sensor-data/VERSION  
      – file exists  
      – exact content (including newline) must be  
        “0.2.2\n”

    • /home/user/projects/clean-sensor-data/CHANGELOG.md  
      – file exists  
      – its very first five non-empty lines must be **exactly** (including
        capitalisation, spaces, dashes and blank line after “# Changelog”):

        # Changelog
        ## [0.2.2] - 2024-05-15
        ### Fixed
        - Handle missing 'temperature' column gracefully (#23)

      – all older changelog entries that were already in the file have to be
        preserved **unchanged** and appear after the new block.

    • /home/user/projects/clean-sensor-data/release_log.txt  
      – file must be created  
      – it must contain **exactly** four lines, each terminated by “\n”:

        OldVersion: 0.2.1
        NewVersion: 0.2.2
        UpdatedFiles:
        - VERSION
        - CHANGELOG.md

      The file will be used by automated tests to verify that you updated the
      correct files.

    Constraints
    -----------
    • You do NOT have sudo rights.  
    • Only touch the VERSION, CHANGELOG.md and release_log.txt files.  
    • Line-endings are Unix style (LF).  
    • Do not add extra spaces, blank lines or Markdown decorations beyond
      what is explicitly requested above.

    Hints
    -----
    1. Use standard CLI tools (echo, sed, printf, etc.) to modify the text
       files; no external packages are needed.  
    2. Prepending to CHANGELOG.md can be done safely with a temporary file or
       with ed/awk/sed tricks—whatever you are comfortable with.  
    3. Make sure you keep the original # Changelog header at the very top,
       followed immediately by the new entry as shown.

    When everything matches the specification, the task is complete.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data for automated tests (DO NOT expose in public task) -->

  <!-- Expected final content of /home/user/projects/clean-sensor-data/VERSION -->
  <file path="/home/user/projects/clean-sensor-data/VERSION">
0.2.2
  </file>

  <!-- Expected first block of /home/user/projects/clean-sensor-data/CHANGELOG.md -->
  <file path="/home/user/projects/clean-sensor-data/CHANGELOG.md" grep="head -n 5">
# Changelog
## [0.2.2] - 2024-05-15
### Fixed
- Handle missing 'temperature' column gracefully (#23)
  </file>

  <!-- Expected complete content of /home/user/projects/clean-sensor-data/release_log.txt -->
  <file path="/home/user/projects/clean-sensor-data/release_log.txt">
OldVersion: 0.2.1
NewVersion: 0.2.2
UpdatedFiles:
- VERSION
- CHANGELOG.md
  </file>
